ddls:
CREATE TABLE "flows" (
  "flow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network flow */,
  "lost_packets" INTEGER /* Number of packets lost during the flow */,
  "tx_packets" INTEGER /* Number of packets transmitted in the flow */,
  "tx_bytes" INTEGER /* Total bytes transmitted in the flow */,
  "tx_offered_mbps" REAL /* Offered transmission rate in Mbps */,
  "rx_packets" INTEGER /* Number of packets received in the flow */,
  "rx_bytes" INTEGER /* Total bytes received in the flow */,
  "throughput_mbps" REAL /* Throughput measured in Mbps */,
  "mean_delay_rx_packets" REAL /* Mean delay for received packets */,
  "mean_jitter" REAL /* Mean jitter during the flow */,
  "packet_loss_ratio" REAL /* Packet loss ratio calculated for the flow */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the network flow */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the network flow */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source device */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the destination device */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network segment where the flow occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flow_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_flows_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES network_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device (e.g., data center, office) */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., router, switch, firewall) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the network segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network segment */,
  "parent_segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent segment (for hierarchical segmentation) */,
  "segment_type" TEXT /* Type of segment (e.g., corporate, R&D, data center) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, admin, support staff) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "flow_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the flow that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high packet loss, low throughput) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message providing context for the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "acknowledged_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who acknowledged the alert */,
  "acknowledged_at" TEXT /* Time when the alert was acknowledged */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report findings */,
  "detailed_report" TEXT /* Detailed report content */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network segment the report is about */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device that underwent maintenance */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the maintenance */,
  "next_scheduled_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_topology" (
  "topology_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topology entry */,
  "source_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source device */,
  "destination_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the destination device */,
  "connection_type" TEXT /* Type of connection between devices (e.g., Ethernet, Wi-Fi) */,
  "connection_speed" TEXT /* Speed of the connection (e.g., 10 Gbps) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the connection (e.g., active, down) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topology_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistics entry */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network segment */,
  "avg_throughput" REAL /* Average throughput for the segment */,
  "avg_packet_loss" REAL /* Average packet loss for the segment */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded statistics */,
  "avg_jitter" REAL /* Average jitter for the segment */,
  "avg_mean_delay" REAL /* Average mean delay for the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id")
)
